mission.title: clusternet: cannot deploy demo application, hub and agent have some error logs
mission.description: cannot deploy demo application, hub and agent have some error logs. This issue affects 0+ users.

steps.title: Identify clusternet troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your clusternet deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n clusternet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusternet
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusternet -n clusternet --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review clusternet configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant clusternet configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n clusternet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusternet
kubectl get configmap -n clusternet -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=clusternet
```
#### What happened:

I use `hack/local-running.sh` to create one parent cluster and three child clusters. But I cannot deploy demo application, there is nothing happened.

#### What you expected to happen:

the demo application is here.

steps.title: Apply the fix for cannot deploy demo application, hub and agent have some
steps.description:
Please help verify below parts.

1. Make sure that all Clusternet components are running correctly. `kubectl get pod -n clusternet-system`.
2. Install `kubectl clusternet` plugin.

    ```bash
    kubectl clusternet get ns
    ```

    Above command should work correctly.

3. Make sure you've
```yaml
> kubectl get base -A
>
```

steps.title: Confirm cannot deploy demo application, hub and agent is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusternet -n clusternet --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n clusternet --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Please help verify below parts.

1. Make sure that all Clusternet components are running correctly. `kubectl get pod -n clusternet-system`.
2. Install `kubectl clusternet` plugin.

    ```bash
    kubectl clusternet get ns
    ```

    Above command should work correctly.

3. Make sure you've updated the `spec.subscribers.clusterAffinity.matchLabels`.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with clusternet installed or the issue environment reproducible.
